title: Kafka Fundamentals Quiz
description: Test your basic knowledge of Kafka topics, partitions, and consumer groups.

questions.question: In Kafka, what guarantees the strict ordering of messages?

options.text: Messages are globally ordered across the entire topic.
Messages are ordered only within a single partition.
Messages are ordered by the consumer group.
Messages are ordered by the Zookeeper node.

questions.question: What happens in a Consumer Group if there are 5 partitions but 6 consumers?

options.text: The 6th consumer sits idle.
Kafka automatically creates a 6th partition.
Two consumers share the load of one partition.
An error is thrown and the consumer group crashes.

questions.question: When does Kafka delete a message from a topic?

options.text: Immediately after it has been read by all consumer groups.
When the topic's retention time or size limit is reached.
When a producer sends a 'delete' signal.
Kafka never deletes messages.

questions.question: How does Kafka decide which partition a message goes to if a message Key is provided?

options.text: It uses Round-Robin assignment.
It hashes the Key to assign it consistently to a specific partition.
It sends it to the partition with the least amount of data.
It broadcasts it to all partitions.

questions.question: What is an Offset in Kafka?

options.text: A unique, sequential ID assigned to each message within a partition.
The time difference between producing and consuming a message.
A pointer to the next broker in the cluster.
The byte array size of a serialized message.
